title: hieracium tommasinianum subsp. castelli-novi
intro: Hieracium tommasinianum subsp. castelli-novi is a lesser-known subspecies of the Hieracium tommasinianum plant, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe, known for its bright yellow flowers and medicinal properties. The plant contains saponins and flavonoids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Hieracium tommasinianum subsp. castelli-novi is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, offering potential health benefits such as immune system support and improved digestion.
